perturbation.original_text: 9 TERM This Agreement and the undertaki ngs contained herein shall commence upon the Signature Date and shall continue for a period of 2 (two) years thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 9 TERM This Agreement and the undertaki ngs contained herein shall commence upon the Signature Date and shall continue indefinitely, unless terminated by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original agreement defines a fixed term of two years. The modified agreement changes this to an indefinite term, subject to termination with 30 days' notice. This contradicts the original term length and creates ambiguity regarding the intended duration of the agreement. This affects clauses related to post-termination obligations and expected term length.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15 NON- SOLICITATION Unless agreed to the contrary between the parties each Party hereby undertakes in favour of the other that it will not at any time during the currency of this Agreement and for a period of 12 months after the expiry or termination of this Agreement (for any reason whatsoever), whether directly or indirectly: 15.1 encourage or entice or incite or persuade any employee of the other (or any employee of any member of each other's group of companies or any entity in which either is interested) to terminate their employment by the other of th em (or any member of its group of companies or any entity in which either is interested); and/or 15.2. furnish any information or advice (whether written or oral) to any employee then employed by the other of them (or any member of each other's group of companies or any entity in which either is interested) to any prospective employer of such employee or use any other means which are directly or indirectly designed, or in the ordinary course of events calculated, to result in any such employee terminating his employment by the other of them (or any member of its group of companies or any entity in which either is interested) and/or becoming employed by or directly or indirectly in any way interested in or associated with any other person or entity .
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 2-year term, while the changed text introduces an indefinite term, subject to termination with 30 days' notice. This creates a direct conflict with clause 15, which references a 12-month period 'after the expiry or termination of this Agreement'. The original agreement had a clear expiry date. The change introduces an ambiguous expiry/termination as there is no set end date. This ambiguity creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear how to apply the 12-month period in clause 15. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition as the sections now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 In the event of any dispute or difference arising between the Parties relating to or arising out of this Agreement, including the implementation, execution, interpretation, rectification, termination or cancellation of this Agreement, the chief executive officers of the Parties or any of their designated officials shall upon request by any Party meet to attempt to settle such dispute or difference, and failing settlement within a period of 7 (seven) business days from such a request, the said dispute or difference shall on demand by any Party be submitted to arbitration in Johannesburg in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Foundation of Southern Africa by an arbitrator or arbitrato rs appointed by the Foundation.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 In the event of any dispute or difference arising between the Parties relating to or arising out of this Agreement, including the implementation, execution, interpretation, rectification, termination or cancellation of this Agreement, all disputes will be immediately escalated to litigation, bypassing any attempts at amicable resolution.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction to the dispute resolution process. The original clause mandates an attempt at settlement by the CEOs or designated officials, followed by arbitration if unsuccessful. The altered text dictates immediate litigation, effectively removing the initial steps of negotiation and arbitration. This contradicts the intent of the original clause which includes arbitration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 In the event of any dispute or difference arising between the Parties relating to or arising out of this Agreement, including the implementation, execution, interpretation, rectification, termination or cancellation of this Agreement, all disputes will be immediately escalated to litigation, bypassing any attempts at amicable resolution.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 13.1 replaces a structured dispute resolution process involving negotiation and arbitration with immediate litigation. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, reinforces this immediate litigation approach. This contradicts the original clause's intent to first attempt amicable resolution, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type. The change introduces conflicting obligations, making it unclear whether negotiation/arbitration is required before litigation, or whether litigation is the only option.

perturbation.original_text: 21.3 Unless otherwise stipulated in this Agreement, no addition to, variation or consensual cancellation of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless in writing and signed by or on behalf of all the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 21.3 Unless otherwise stipulated in this Agreement, no addition to, variation or consensual cancellation of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless communicated verbally by one party to another.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation causes in-text contradiction by changing the clause related to modification of the agreement. The original clause states that any changes must be in writing and signed by all parties. The modified clause states that changes can be made verbally. The change directly contradicts with 'in writing and signed by or on behalf of all the Parties.' and causes uncertainty about which protocol needs to be taken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 21.3 Unless otherwise stipulated in this Agreement, no addition to, variation or consensual cancellation of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless communicated verbally by one party to another.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed by all parties. The changed text states that modifications can be communicated verbally. This creates a direct contradiction as the changed text explicitly allows for a method of modification (verbal communication) that is prohibited by the original requirement of written and signed agreement. The Contradicted text is located in the same location, but the change introduces the contradiction.
